intro: Calamagrostis elongata, also known as Mountain Fescue, is a perennial grass species native to mountainous regions in Eastern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis elongata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, Calamagrostis elongata is often prepared as a tea or decoction, which may also be available in capsule or tablet form for the treatment of various health conditions.
